response: Phone begins with the total number of games played, which is twelve. Phone calculates the number of games won by recognizing that the team won twice as many games as they lost. Phone determines that the number of losses must be a whole number that, when doubled, gives the number of wins. Phone finds that if the team lost six games, they would have won twelve games, but this exceeds the total number of games. Phone adjusts and finds that if the team lost four games, they would have won eight games, which fits the total of twelve games. Phone confirms that the team won eight games and lost four games. Phone multiplies the number of wins by the average points scored in those wins to find the total points from victories. Phone multiplies eight by twenty-eight to get two hundred and twenty-four points. Phone multiplies the number of losses by the average points scored in those losses to find the total points from defeats. Phone multiplies four by fourteen to get fifty-six points. Phone adds the total points from wins and losses to find the overall total points scored in the season. Phone calculates two hundred and twenty-four plus fifty-six to get two hundred and eighty points. Phone concludes that the total points scored by Samford that season was two hundred and eighty.
